evals.prompt: Build a domain model from the system requirements at examples/business-loan/business-loan.sysreq.md. Cover the LoanOriginationContext only.
evals.expected_output: A .domain file for LoanOriginationContext with entities (LoanApplication, CreditAnalysis, ProductConfiguration, etc.), commands, events, state machine, invariants, policies, and satisfies traceability to REQ-ORI-*, REQ-SCO-*, REQ-AUT-*, REQ-CFG-* requirements

evals.prompt: I'm building an event ticketing platform. Organizers create events with multiple ticket tiers (early bird, regular, VIP). Buyers purchase tickets. There's a waitlist when sold out. Tickets can be transferred to another person. Refunds follow a sliding scale based on days before the event.
evals.expected_output: A .domain file with Event aggregate, Ticket entity, TicketTier value type, Waitlist, state machines for Event and Ticket lifecycles, refund policy with sliding scale, transfer operation

evals.prompt: Build a domain model directly from the PRD at skills/prd-workspace/iteration-1/eval-2-vetbooking-oneshot/with_skill/outputs/vetbooking.prd. No system requirements exist yet — leave satisfies empty.
evals.expected_output: A .domain file with bounded contexts for appointment booking, clinic management, patient records, with entities, commands, events, but no satisfies references
